text: A 12mm working trocar is inserted in the left infraumbilical position along the medial clavicular line, and a 5mm trocar is placed on the epigastrium to the right of the midline. The optical trocar is inserted using an open technique in the infraumbilical position.

The surgeon uses an ultra shear to separate the omentum from the transverse colon, opening the omental bursa widely. This exposes a cystic tumor on the tail of the pancreas, measuring ten centimeters in diameter. Adhesions are carefully severed from the dorsal and retropancreatic directions, and a flexible retractor is inserted through a five millimeter trocar to aid in positioning the tumor for further dissection. The connection to the splenic hilus is severed step by step.

The surgeon identifies the splenic vein on the dorsal surface of the cyst and carefully severs the small branch veins between the pancreas and the splenic vein. The pancreatic branches and branches of the splenic artery are severed on the portion of the pancreas to be removed. The endless stapler, loaded with a musculoskeletal cartridge, is applied to resect the tail of the pancreas and the cystic tumor. Remaining small adhesions are severed using the ultra shear.

The surgeon rinses and cleans the tumor bed, then seals the cut edge of the tail of the pancreas with fibrin adhesive to protect against fistulas. The tumor is removed through the infraumbilical trocar opening using an appropriately sized bag.
